project_identity.name: LinterProject
project_identity.primary_goal: Analyze Java code for style issues, principle violations, and design-pattern candidates using both source and bytecode analysis.

summary.one_line: A modular Java linter with CLI + Swing GUI, configurable rules, and ASM-powered bytecode pattern detection.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates practical static-analysis architecture beyond simple regex linting.
Combines source-level checks with bytecode-level structural analysis.
Supports configuration-driven behavior and multiple user interfaces (CLI + GUI).

libraries.name: ASM
libraries.purpose: Bytecode parsing and structural analysis of compiled .class files

keys.name: enabled_linters
keys.semantics: Either 'all' or comma-separated linter class simple names

design_patterns_used: Dependency Injection for shared services (ASMReader, LCOMCalculator)
Strategy-like selection by file type (LinterRunner type buckets)

cli: Create enabled linter instances using factory
Split by file type (.class vs non-.class)
Run matching linter sets and aggregate output
gui: Load all registered linters into linter dropdown
Filter suggested file choices by selected linter input type
Edit/load/save config values in GUI panel
Run lint asynchronously (SwingWorker) and display result

linters.name: SnakeLinter
linters.logic: Regex-based checks for snake_case violations in class names, fields, and variables

linters.name: LeastKnowledgePrincipleLinter
linters.logic: Law of Demeter heuristics using chained-call/chained-access regex patterns, with comment/literal handling
linters.output: file path + violating lines + total count

linters.name: TrailingWhitespaceLinter
linters.logic: Flags lines ending with space or tab
linters.output: file path + list of lines + total count

linters.name: UnusedImportLinter
linters.logic: Collect explicit non-static imports, then detect missing token usage by simple-name regex

linters.name: PublicNonFinalFieldLinter
linters.logic: Flags fields that are public and non-final

linters.name: SRPLinter
linters.logic: Calculates LCOM4; flags classes with LCOM >= threshold and reports disconnected method components
linters.output: detailed SRP report with cohesion metrics and suggested component split

linters.name: FacadePatternLinter
linters.logic: Detects candidates by delegated public methods and interactions with multiple internal subsystem classes
linters.output: candidate class + delegation ratio + subsystem types

linters.name: StrategyPatternLinter
linters.logic: Looks for interface calls with used non-void returns and evidence of multiple concrete strategy implementors

linters.name: SingletonPatternLinter
linters.logic: Private constructors + static self-typed field + public static self-returning accessor loading that field

linters.name: DecoratorPatternLinter
linters.logic: Type extends/implements a target type and stores same type in a field

linters.name: AdapterPatternLinter
linters.logic: Target type relationship + adaptee field + delegated method calls to adaptee owner

linters.name: BooleanFlagMethodLinter
linters.logic: Flags methods with boolean or Boolean parameters

linters.name: TooManyParametersLinter
linters.logic: Flags methods/constructors where argument count exceeds configured limit

linters.name: DesignRiskLinter
linters.logic: Weighted score from class size, coupling, mutable static state, and low cohesion
linters.output: class risk breakdown + total score + risk level

linters.name: PlantUMLGenerator
linters.logic: Produces PlantUML class/interface definitions and relationships

metric_engine.name: LCOMCalculator
approach: Build method->field access graph from bytecode instructions
Use component count as cohesion score

testing.harness: Dynamic compilation of temporary Java fixtures into .class files
covered_areas: Least Knowledge / Law of Demeter detection
testing.notable_repo_detail: LeastKnowledgePrincipleLinterTest appears in two packages (domain and test).

features: Filter compatible file suggestions by linter input type
Async lint run with status timing
Result pane with active settings summary
ux_notes: Linter dropdown is intentionally populated with all registered linters.
Input path selection determines actual analysis scope for each run.

strengths: Hybrid analysis (source + bytecode) in one tool
Modular rule architecture with single linter interface
Both terminal and desktop GUI workflows
Runnable fat-jar packaging for easy demos
Automated tests with dynamic fixture generation
limitations_and_tradeoffs: Some rules are heuristic and may produce false positives/negatives
Not all implemented linters have direct dedicated tests yet
Some output formatting has encoding artifacts in certain rule messages
No CI workflow files currently present

portfolio_positioning.problem_statement: Build a practical Java linter to detect design issues and anti-patterns for grading and code-review scenarios.
engineering_highlights: Rule registration and dependency wiring via factory
